sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
அன்பர் அழைத்தவுடன் ஆர்வமாய் ஓடி வந்து
துன்பம் துடைத்திடும் தூயவனே மதிசேகரன் மைந்தனே
sections.translit:
anbar azhaitthavudan ArvamAy Odi vandhu
thunbam thudaitthidum thUyavanE madhisEkaran maindhanE
sections.en: Oh, immaculate lord and son of Chandrasekara, please come running when your dear devotees call, and remove all their obstacles.

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
மங்கள நீராடிய மகேஸ்வரியின் காவலனாய் நின்றாய்
விக்கி நின்றதோர் கிழவனுக்குத் துணை செய்ய மதயானையாய் மாறினாய்
சங்கம் அணிந்த ரங்கனுக்கு பள்ளி அமைக்க கள்ளச் சிறுவன் ஆனாய்
கமண்டலம் கவிழ்த்தே காவிரி அருளிட கரைந்திடும் காகமானாய்
sections.translit:
mangaLa nIrAdiya magEswariyin kAvalanAy nindRAy
vikki nindRadhOr kizhavanukkuth thuNai seyya madhayAnaiyAi mArinAy
sangam aNindha ranganukku paLLi amaikka kaLLach chiruvan AnAy
kamaNdalam kavizhtthE kAviri aruLida karaindhidum kAgamAnAy
sections.en: You stood as a watchman and served as a guard when Goddess Maheswari took an auspicious bath. You transformed yourself into a mad elephant to help the old man who stood in a millet field with non-stop hiccups. You became a mischievous little lad with a precise intent to make a temple for the conch wielding Lord Ranganatha. With an intent to deliver the divine river Cauveri/Kaveri to this universe by overturning a water jug of a saint, you became a cawing crow.

sections.label: Charanam
sections.ta:
பற்பல விதமாய்த் தோன்றினும் பரிபூர்ண குணபதியே
நற்றமிழ்க் கவி பாடியே எந்நேரமும் துதிபாடி நின்
பொற்பதம் பிடித்த ஏழையென் ஏவலிதே
அற்பவினைகளைக்  களைவாய் கற்பகத்தருவே
sections.translit:
paRpala vidhamAyth thOndRinum paripUrNa guNapathiyE
natRamizhk kavi pAdiyE ennEramum thudhipAdi nin
poRpadham pidittha Ezhaiyen EvalidhE
aRpavinaigaLaik kaLaivAy kaRpagattharuvE
sections.en: This humble soul who relishes in singing your glory in chaste Tamil and constantly worships your golden feet has only a menial request for you. I know that you are like the celestial tree that grants all boons to favorites. Please rid me of my silly past evil deeds and bless me. Author’s Note The anupallavi describes the various mystical acts or leelas of Ganesha including his birth, based on mythological legends. The origin of Ganesha from the turmeric paste adorned by Goddess Parvathi, and his standing guard to his mother’s chamber during her bath are popular stories. Turmeric paste story Ganesha becoming a fierce elephant to compel Valli to marry his brother Muruga is detailed in Skandapuranam. Ganesha appearing as a mischievous boy to install Srirangam temple is detailed in Legends of Tiruchirapalli. Ganesha & Vibhishana Ganesha’s mischief to bring the river Kaveri (Cauvery) from Sage Agasthiya’s kamandalam or water pot in the form of a crow is in legends associated with ThalaKaveri Agastheeswarar temple sthalapuranam. Ganesha and thalacauveri Ganesha and kaveri Ganesha’s efforts to bring about the flow of a river in the drought stricken south at that time is described in the reference below. Ganesha removes drought
